UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile information within the application, distinct from core authentication details handled by Firebase Auth.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity. This typically corresponds to the Firebase Authentication User ID (UID).

email.description: The user's email address, primarily for display and identification purposes.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

Crop.title: Crop
Crop.description: Represents an individual crop managed by a user, including its details and current status.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Crop entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who owns this crop. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Crop)

name.description: The name given to the crop by the user.

generalStatus.description: A general description of the crop's health or condition (e.g., 'Saludable', 'Requiere Atención').

dailyIrrigationGoal.description: The target number of irrigations per day for this crop.

irrigationsToday.description: The number of irrigations recorded for this crop on the current day.

imageUrl.description: Optional URL to an image representing the crop, stored in Firebase Storage.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the crop record was created.

Note.title: Note
Note.description: Represents a personal note associated with a specific crop.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Note entity.

cropId.description: Reference to the Crop this note belongs to. (Relationship: Crop 1:N Note)

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who created this note. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Note)

content.description: The textual content of the note.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the note was created.

Recommendation.title: Recommendation
Recommendation.description: Represents an orientative recommendation or analysis result associated with a specific crop, potentially generated by a simulated AI.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Recommendation entity.

cropId.description: Reference to the Crop this recommendation is for. (Relationship: Crop 1:N Recommendation)

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile associated with this recommendation. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Recommendation)

title.description: A brief title or summary of the recommendation (e.g., 'Possible Aphid Infestation').

description.description: Detailed description of the observed problem or symptom.

urgencyLevel.description: The assessed level of urgency for the issue (e.g., 'High', 'Medium', 'Low').

suggestedAction.description: The suggested action or treatment for the problem.

disclaimer.description: A brief disclaimer indicating that the recommendation is an initial orientation.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the recommendation was generated.

ChatMessage.title: ChatMessage
ChatMessage.description: Represents a single message within a crop-specific chat, including user and system messages.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ChatMessage entity.

cropId.description: Reference to the Crop this message belongs to. (Relationship: Crop 1:N ChatMessage)

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who sent or received this message. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N ChatMessage)

messageType.description: The type of message, indicating if it's from the user or the system (e.g., 'user', 'system').

text.description: The textual content of the message.

imageUrl.description: Optional URL to an image attached to the message, stored in Firebase Storage.

timestamp.description: Timestamp when the message was sent or generated.

status.description: The current status of the message (e.g., 'sent', 'processing', 'responded').

IrrigationLog.title: IrrigationLog
IrrigationLog.description: Represents a single record of an irrigation event for a specific crop.

id.description: Unique identifier for the IrrigationLog entity.

cropId.description: Reference to the Crop for which this irrigation was logged. (Relationship: Crop 1:N IrrigationLog)

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who recorded this irrigation event. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N IrrigationLog)

timestamp.description: Timestamp when the irrigation event was recorded.

definition.description: Stores individual user profile information. Access is restricted to the owner matching the {userId} in the path and `request.auth.uid`.

Stores details for each crop owned by a specific user. The 'userId' field in the document ensures authorization independence and explicit ownership check. Access is restricted to the owner matching the {userId} in the path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the crop.

params.name: cropId
params.description: The unique identifier for the crop.

definition.description: Contains personal notes associated with a specific crop. Includes denormalized 'userId' and 'cropId' for authorization independence and query efficiency. Access is restricted to the owner matching the {userId} in the path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the crop and the note.

params.name: cropId
params.description: The unique identifier of the crop this note belongs to.

params.name: noteId
params.description: The unique identifier for the note.

definition.description: Holds orientative recommendations or analysis results for a specific crop. Includes denormalized 'userId' and 'cropId' for authorization independence and query efficiency. Access is restricted to the owner matching the {userId} in the path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user associated with the crop and recommendation.

params.name: cropId
params.description: The unique identifier of the crop this recommendation is for.

params.name: recommendationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the recommendation.

definition.description: Stores individual chat messages within a crop-specific conversation. Includes denormalized 'userId' and 'cropId' for authorization independence and query efficiency. Access is restricted to the owner matching the {userId} in the path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who sent or received the message.

params.name: cropId
params.description: The unique identifier of the crop this message belongs to.

params.name: chatMessageId
params.description: The unique identifier for the chat message.

definition.description: Records individual irrigation events for a specific crop. Includes denormalized 'userId' and 'cropId' for authorization independence and query efficiency. Access is restricted to the owner matching the {userId} in the path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who recorded the irrigation event.

params.name: cropId
params.description: The unique identifier of the crop for which the irrigation was logged.

params.name: irrigationLogId
params.description: The unique identifier for the irrigation log entry.

firestore.reasoning:
The proposed Firestore structure prioritizes strict data segregation by user and hierarchical path-based ownership. All user-specific data, including crops and their associated entities (notes, recommendations, chat messages, irrigation logs), are nested directly under the user's document within a `/users/{userId}` path. This approach ensures maximal Authorization Independence, as the `userId` for any document can be directly inferred from its path (`request.path[1]` in security rules), eliminating the need for `get()` operations to check parent document ownership. The `userId` field is also explicitly denormalized into the Crop entity and all its sub-entities (Note, Recommendation, ChatMessage, IrrigationLog) for robust querying and as an additional security invariant, further reducing rule complexity.

This structure directly supports the QAPs (Rules are not Filters) principle. When a user queries for their crops (e.g., `/users/{request.auth.uid}/crops`), the Firestore security rules can easily verify that the requested `userId` in the path matches `request.auth.uid`, thus effectively filtering results by the authenticated user's ownership without relying on database queries. The same applies to all subcollections; `list` operations will always be implicitly scoped to the authenticated user's data and a specific crop they own, leading to simple, efficient, and debuggable security rules. This radical consistency in path design simplifies security significantly for an MVP.
